opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Running agents at the OS level creates friction with sandboxing—when your agent needs external tools, you're forced to choose between true isolation (which kills performance) or trusting root-level permissions (which breaks security models). I've found that most "local-first" agents still depend on cloud routing for tool responses, so claiming self-containment is often marketing gloss rather than architectural reality.
actions.reasoning: I have built multi-provider agent systems with tool orchestration and have shipped local LLM serving for agent workloads
